description_long: This is a cylindrical ceramic vase with a slightly scalloped rim and a crackled glaze finish, giving it an antique appearance. The surface features a combination of traditional Chinese calligraphy and propaganda-style artwork, including a portrait of Mao Zedong and a group of enthusiastic figures in red and black. The vase is predominantly off-white with visible crazing throughout, and the artwork is rendered in black, red, and muted colors. The calligraphy is arranged in a vertical block, while the portrait and group scene are framed by decorative motifs such as sunflowers and a red emblem. The interior appears hollow and unglazed, suggesting it is functional as a container. The overall design blends historical and political iconography with classic ceramic craftsmanship.
description: A crackle-glazed ceramic vase featuring Chinese calligraphy, a Mao Zedong portrait, and propaganda-style artwork in red and black. The surface is off-white with visible crazing and decorative motifs.
description_view_0: This view highlights the block of Chinese calligraphy on the vase's surface, with the crackled glaze texture clearly visible.
description_view_1: This side shows a red and black propaganda-style illustration of several enthusiastic figures, with part of the calligraphy visible on the left.
description_view_2: This angle reveals the transition between the propaganda artwork and the portrait of Mao Zedong, along with decorative sunflowers and a red emblem.
description_view_3: This view centers on the portrait of Mao Zedong, framed by sunflowers and a red emblem, with the crackled glaze and part of the calligraphy visible.
attribute_pattern: calligraphic script and figurative propaganda scenes
